event: history of MRSA impetigo at the forearm
cultures from the surgical site were positive for MRSA
radiograph of both hips in anterior-posterior view was unremarkable
treatment with intravenous (IV) antibiotics that work against MRSA to treat cellulitis
Positron emission tomography-computed tomography (PET-CT) scan conducted
blood cultures were positive for MRSA bacteria
clinical picture appropriate for fulminant MRSA sepsis
admitted to the intensive care unit (ICU)
further enlargement of the abscesses diameter
patient's general health condition continued to deteriorate
combined approach of Smith-Peterson and modified Stoppa was chosen
general anaesthesia with muscle relaxant was administered
drainage and debridement of the obturator internus muscle (OIM)
drainage of the lateral side of the abscess
adductor brevis muscle debridement and lavage
additional antibiotic treatment support for 6 weeks
